content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 9,
1992
Joyce Ann Felando
Treasurer for Friends of Felando
c/o Assemblymember Gerald N.
Felando
State Capitol
Sacramento,
CA 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—92—385
Dear Ms.
Felando:
This is in reply to your letter dated June 23,
1992 which
concerns two issues:
(1)
Whether your husband,
Assemblyinember Gerald Felando,
can
transfer his campaign funds to a new checking account but
leave some of the funds in the old checking account for a
short period to cover outstanding checks;
and
(2)
Whether the Felando campaign may submit its campaign
reporting forms in the computerized format that accompanied
your letter.
On June 16,
1992
I informed Assemblymember Felando that it
was permissible for him to transfer his campaign funds into a new
campaign checking account but leave some of the funds in the old
account to cover outstanding checks.
Further,
Asseinblymember
Felando agreed that the old account would remain open for
a
day
period solely to allow outstanding checks written against that
account to clear and for no other reason.
Assemblymember Felando
indicated that he felt this was a reasonable resolution to the
problem posed by Section 85201.
That section permits only
campaign bank account for each public office sought or held.
Also,
please note that Mr.
Felando must file with the
Commission a Form 502
campaign bank account statement both within
10 days after he opens his new account and after he terminates his
old account.
I have enclosed two 502 forms for your use in this
regard.
Finally,
as to your request concerning submission of Mr.
Felando’s campaign reports in
a specific computerized format,
the
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660


Our File No.
A-92-385
Page
2
forms you submitted are being reviewed by Kim Taylor in our
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division.
She will contact you
soon after she has completed her review.
If you have any questions, please contact Carla Wardlow, the
Chief of the our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division, at
322—5662 and she will be able to assist you.
Sincerely,
Way
Ordos
Executive Director

sections.question: Whether your husband, Assemblymember Gerald Felando, can transfer his campaign funds to a new checking account but leave some of the funds in the old checking account for a short period to cover outstanding checks; and (2) Whether the Felando campaign may submit its campaign reporting forms in the computerized format that accompanied your letter.
sections.conclusion: On June 16, 1992 I informed Assemblymember Felando that it was permissible for him to transfer his campaign funds into a new campaign checking account but leave some of the funds in the old account to cover outstanding checks. Further, Assemblymember Felando agreed that the old account would remain open for a day period solely to allow outstanding checks written against that account to clear and for no other reason. Also, please note that Mr. Felando must file with the Commission a Form 502 campaign bank account statement both within 10 days after he opens his new account and after he terminates his old account.
sections.question_synthetic: Can an assemblymember transfer campaign funds to a new account while maintaining an old account temporarily for outstanding checks, and can campaign reports be submitted in a specific computerized format?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC approved transferring campaign funds to a new account while keeping the old account open briefly (for one day) to clear outstanding checks, provided Assemblymember Felando files Form 502 statements within 10 days of opening the new account and terminating the old account. The question regarding computerized reporting format submission was referred to the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for review.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Specific file number A-92-385. References prior communication dated June 16, 1992. Secondary issue regarding computerized format was deferred to another division rather than fully addressed in this letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether your husband, Assemblymember Gerald Felando, can transfer his campaign funds to a new checking account but leave some of the funds in the old checking account for a short period to cover outstanding checks; and (2) Whether the Felando campaign may submit its campaign reporting forms in the computerized format that accompanied your letter.

CONCLUSION: On June 16, 1992 I informed Assemblymember Felando that it was permissible for him to transfer his campaign funds into a new campaign checking account but leave some of the funds in the old account to cover outstanding checks. Further, Assemblymember Felando agreed that the old account would remain open for a day period solely to allow outstanding checks written against that account to clear and for no other reason. Also, please note that Mr. Felando must file with the Commission a Form 502 campaign bank account statement both within 10 days after he opens his new account and after he terminates his old account.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission July 9, 1992 Joyce Ann Felando Treasurer for Friends of Felando c/o Assemblymember Gerald N. Felando State Capitol Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A—92—385 Dear Ms. Felando: This is in reply to your letter dated June 23, 1992 which concerns two issues: (1) Whether your husband, Assemblyinember Gerald Felando, can transfer his campaign funds to a new checking account but leave some of the funds in the old checking account for a short period to cover outstanding checks; and (2) Whether the Felando campaign may submit its campaign reporting forms in the computerized format that accompanied your letter. On June 16, 1992 I informed Assemblymember Felando that it was permissible for him to transfer his campaign funds into a new campaign checking account but leave some of the funds in the old account to cover outstanding checks. Further, Asseinblymember Felando agreed that the old account would remain open for a day period solely to allow outstanding checks written against that account to clear and for no other reason. Assemblymember Felando indicated that he felt this was a reasonable resolution to the problem posed by Section 85201. That section permits only campaign bank account for each public office sought or held. Also, please note that Mr. Felando must file with the Commission a Form 502 campaign bank account statement both within 10 days after he opens his new account and after he terminates his old account. I have enclosed two 502 forms for your use in this regard. Finally, as to your request concerning submission of Mr. Felando’s campaign reports in a specific computerized format, the 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660 Our File No. A-92-385 Page 2 forms you submitted are being reviewed by Kim Taylor in our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division. She will contact you soon after she has completed her review. If you have any questions, please contact Carla Wardlow, the Chief of the our Technical Assistance and Analysis Division, at 322—5662 and she will be able to assist you. Sincerely, Way Ordos Executive Director
embedding.summary: The FPPC advised Assemblymember Gerald Felando that he may transfer campaign funds to a new checking account while maintaining the old account temporarily to cover outstanding checks, with required Form 502 filings within 10 days. The question regarding computerized campaign report submission format was referred to the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division.
